figures.caption: Fig. 1. Architecture of the TA-Mem framework. The memory extractor first completes offline memory processing to semantically chunk the conversation dialogues and extract episodic information. Then, a database constructor stores and indexes the memory page through different keys and embeddings. The database consists of multiple query interfaces, allowing it to integrate with the tools of a retrieval agent. The retriever reasons on user input questions by running an agentic loop with tools to autonomously explore the memory database and precisely locate the relevant memory context for model inference.
Fig. 3. Success rate, F1, BLEU-1, and token usage vs iteration budgets.
